title: Jeff Bezos is pushing an AI-first Prime Video redesign, and studios may end up paying for it
excerpt: Jeff Bezos has found a new place to advertise Amazon’s artificial intelligence, and it happens to be the screen where you were hoping to watch a film. Amazon’s executive chairman is pushing a redesign of Prime Video, known internally as Lighthouse, meant to turn the streaming service into a high-profile showcase for the hundreds of […]
content_html:
Jeff Bezos has found a new place to advertise Amazon’s artificial intelligence, and it happens to be the screen where you were hoping to watch a film.

Amazon’s executive chairman is pushing a redesign of Prime Video, known internally as Lighthouse, meant to turn the streaming service into a high-profile showcase for the hundreds of billions of dollars the company has spent on AI, Reuters reported on Thursday.

It is the same instinct that recently put Alexa inside the search bar on Amazon’s shopping site.

The work is led by Prime Video head Mike Hopkins, with the unit’s global design lead, Kam Keshmiri, running the project day to day.

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

The problem it is chasing, helping people find something to watch without endless scrolling, is the same one Netflix has thrown generative AI at, and it gained urgency after Bezos pushed back at an internal presentation, telling the team its plans did not lean hard enough on AI.

Bezos has since been taking regular updates on the work, according to the reporting, and an earlier set of designs was scrapped once the team was told to start again with AI at the centre.

That level of founder attention is unusual for a product feature, and it tells you how much weight Amazon is placing on being seen to have something to show for its spending.

According to the reporting, Lighthouse would rebuild the home screen around machine-generated recommendations rather than a fixed grid.

Features under development include AI-generated thumbnails, pre-filled browsing prompts such as “1980s action films” and “Christmas rom-coms”, and voice search powered by Alexa. Conventional search would stay, for anyone who already knows what they want.

The change carries a commercial edge that studios will notice. Prime Video currently sells fixed, premium placement slots on its home screen, the digital equivalent of an end-cap in a supermarket.

Lighthouse would trim those in favour of algorithmic ranking, which unsettles the arrangements studios have relied on to buy their way into a viewer’s eyeline.

In practice, that turns a guaranteed placement into a contest an algorithm decides. Studios that once paid for a fixed spot would instead be ranked alongside everything else on offer, with the personalisation engine choosing who sees what.

For partners used to negotiating visibility, a marketplace they cannot fully see into is a harder thing to plan around.

The prize is reach. Prime Video claims more than 200 million monthly viewers, yet it drew roughly 4.2% of US television viewing time in recent figures cited by Reuters, well behind YouTube on about 13.4% and Netflix on close to 7.8%, and behind Disney+ too.

A home screen seen that often is a useful billboard for a company trying to prove its AI spending has a point.

None of this is Amazon’s first pass at the idea. A 2024 redesign already added “Made for You” collections and synopses trimmed by large language models running on AWS, and the company has been folding generative AI into recaps and dubbing for a while.

The technology has also proved legally awkward, as the Nova Reel lawsuit brought by a group of YouTubers made plain.

The push also sits alongside Amazon’s broader appetite for automation. The company is using AI to speed up film and television production through a small studio team run by executive Albert Cheng, which has already leaned on the technology for battle scenes in the biblical series House of David.

The same company has cut about 30,000 corporate jobs since October, citing AI among the reasons, and the bet that reshaped Alexa is now being aimed squarely at the living room.

Amazon is testing versions of Lighthouse with a selection of users, and the final layout has not been settled. Amazon declined to comment.

What Bezos wants is clear enough anyway, which is for the recommendation engine, not the catalogue, to be the first thing you see.

It is the same instinct that recently put Alexa inside the search bar on Amazon’s shopping site. The work is led by Prime Video head Mike Hopkins, with the unit’s global design lead, Kam Keshmiri, running the project day to day. blocks.html_en: The push also sits alongside Amazon’s broader appetite for automation. The company is using AI to speed up film and television production through a small studio team run by executive Albert Cheng, which has already leaned on the technology for battle scenes in the biblical series House of David.

sentences.en: The push also sits alongside Amazon’s broader appetite for automation.
sentences.zh: 这一举措也符合亚马逊对自动化更广泛的需求。

sentences.en: The company is using AI to speed up film and television production through a small studio team run by executive Albert Cheng, which has already leaned on the technology for battle scenes in the biblical series House of David.
sentences.zh: 该公司正在通过高管 Albert Cheng 管理的一个小型工作室团队，利用人工智能来加快电影和电视制作速度，该团队已经在圣经系列剧《大卫之家》中的战斗场景中使用了该技术。

blocks.html_en: The same company has cut about 30,000 corporate jobs since October, citing AI among the reasons, and the bet that reshaped Alexa is now being aimed squarely at the living room.

sentences.zh: 自 10 月份以来，该公司已裁员约​​ 30,000 个，其中原因之一是人工智能，而重塑 Alexa 的赌注现在正瞄准客厅。

blocks.html_en: Amazon is testing versions of Lighthouse with a selection of users, and the final layout has not been settled. Amazon declined to comment.

sentences.en: Amazon is testing versions of Lighthouse with a selection of users, and the final layout has not been settled.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊正在针对部分用户测试 Lighthouse 版本，最终布局尚未确定。

sentences.en: Amazon declined to comment.
sentences.zh: 亚马逊拒绝置评。

blocks.html_en: What Bezos wants is clear enough anyway, which is for the recommendation engine, not the catalogue, to be the first thing you see.

sentences.en: What Bezos wants is clear enough anyway, which is for the recommendation engine, not the catalogue, to be the first thing you see.
sentences.zh: 不管怎样，贝索斯想要的东西已经很清楚了，那就是让推荐引擎，而不是目录，成为你首先看到的东西。

content_html_zh:
杰夫·贝佐斯找到了一个宣传亚马逊人工智能的新地方，而它恰好是你希望观看电影的屏幕。

据路透社周四报道，亚马逊执行董事长正在推动 Prime Video 的重新设计，内部称为 Lighthouse，旨在将流媒体服务转变为该公司在人工智能上花费的数千亿美元的高调展示。

出于同样的本能，最近将 Alexa 放入亚马逊购物网站的搜索栏中。

这项工作由 Prime Video 负责人 Mike Hopkins 领导，该部门的全球设计负责人 Kam Keshmiri 负责该项目的日常运营。

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

它正在解决的问题是帮助人们找到可以观看的内容，而无需无休止地滚动，这与 Netflix 投入生成式人工智能的问题相同，而在贝索斯在一次内部演示中反驳并告诉团队其计划对人工智能的依赖不够之后，它变得更加紧迫。

据报道，贝索斯此后一直在定期更新工作，当团队被告知以人工智能为中心重新开始时，早期的一组设计就被废弃了。

对于一项产品功能来说，创始人的如此关注程度是不寻常的，它告诉你亚马逊对于让别人看到其支出有什么可展示的重视程度。

据报道，Lighthouse 将围绕机器生成的建议而不是固定网格重建主屏幕。

正在开发的功能包括人工智能生成的缩略图、预填充的浏览提示（例如“1980 年代动作电影”和“圣诞浪漫喜剧”）以及由 Alexa 提供支持的语音搜索。 对于那些已经知道自己想要什么的人来说，传统的搜索将被保留。

这一变化带来了工作室会注意到的商业优势。 Prime Video 目前在其主屏幕上销售固定的优质广告位，相当于超市的数字终端。

Lighthouse 会削减那些有利于算法排名的内容，这扰乱了工作室赖以进入观众视线的安排。

在实践中，这将有保证的排名变成了算法决定的竞赛。 曾经为固定位置付费的工作室将与提供的其他所有内容一起排名，并由个性化引擎选择谁可以看到什么。

对于习惯于谈判可见性的合作伙伴来说，他们无法完全了解的市场是更难规划的事情。

奖品已达。 Prime Video 声称每月有超过 2 亿观众，但根据路透社最近引用的数据，它约占美国电视观看时间的 4.2%，远远落后于 YouTube 的约 13.4% 和 Netflix 的近 7.8%，也落后于 Disney+。

对于试图证明其人工智能支出有意义的公司来说，主屏幕通常是一个有用的广告牌。

这并不是亚马逊第一次尝试这个想法。 2024 年的重新设计已经添加了由 AWS 上运行的大型语言模型修剪的“为您而制作”的集合和概要，并且该公司已经将生成式 AI 融入摘要和配音中一段时间​​了。

事实证明，这项技术在法律上也很尴尬，正如一群 YouTube 用户提起的 Nova Reel 诉讼所表明的那样。

这一举措也符合亚马逊对自动化更广泛的需求。 该公司正在通过高管 Albert Cheng 管理的一个小型工作室团队，利用人工智能来加快电影和电视制作速度，该团队已经在圣经系列剧《大卫之家》中的战斗场景中使用了该技术。

自 10 月份以来，该公司已裁员约​​ 30,000 个，其中原因之一是人工智能，而重塑 Alexa 的赌注现在正瞄准客厅。

亚马逊正在针对部分用户测试 Lighthouse 版本，最终布局尚未确定。 亚马逊拒绝置评。

不管怎样，贝索斯想要的东西已经很清楚了，那就是让推荐引擎，而不是目录，成为你首先看到的东西。
